title: Stalling & Repeating
concept: Identifying loops where the agent retries the exact same failing action expecting a different outcome.

exercises.prompt: What happens when an AI tries to fix itself after a minor critique?

options.text: It over-corrects and changes its solution unnecessarily.
It learns from the critique and improves immediately.

exercises.explain: An AI might over-correct after a critique by changing more than necessary, which can lead to ineffective solutions.

cards.title: Identify Stalling
cards.body: Stalling is when an AI keeps trying the same action, even if it didn’t work before. It’s like casting a fishing line in the same spot over and over, hoping for fish to suddenly appear.

cards.title: What is Stalling & Repeating?
cards.body: Stalling & Repeating happens when an agent continually tries the same approach, expecting a different result, even when it's clear that it won’t work.

cards.title: Looping in Action
cards.body: Imagine you’re stuck in a roundabout, circling the same way without ever finding an exit. That’s what an agent does when it stalls and repeats actions!

cards.title: An Example of Stalling
cards.body: If an AI is trying to unlock a door by turning the doorknob that’s stuck, and it keeps turning it the same way thinking it will eventually open, it’s stalling.

cards.title: Common Misconception
cards.body: Many people think that persistence always works. However, just repeating the same action doesn't guarantee success if the original issue hasn't changed.

cards.title: Stalling vs. Progressing
cards.body: Understand the difference between stalling and making progress!

exercises.prompt: What does it mean when an AI is stalling?

options.text: The AI is trying the same failing action repeatedly.
The AI is learning from its past mistakes.
The AI is switching to a different strategy.

exercises.explain: Stalling refers to an AI that continues to retry the same action without changing its approach, even when it’s not working.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An AI stalling means it is successfully solving problems.
exercises.explain: When an AI is stalling, it is not solving problems effectively; it is stuck in a loop.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: Stalling occurs when an agent tries the same ______, expecting it to work differently.
exercises.sentence: Stalling occurs when an agent tries the same action, expecting it to work differently.
exercises.explain: The correct answer is 'action'. Stalling involves repeating the same action expecting different outcomes.

exercises.prompt: Which of these is an example of stalling?

options.text: An AI trying different keys to unlock a door.
An AI repeatedly trying to unlock a door with the same broken key.
An AI learning from past mistakes and adjusting its strategy.

exercises.explain: An AI stalling means it keeps using the same ineffective key hoping it will suddenly work.

exercises.prompt: In which situation is an AI demonstrably stalling?

options.text: Revising a plan after receiving feedback.
Switching strategies to find a new solution.
Attempting to cross a river by swimming at the same spot after failing multiple times.
Asking for help to find alternate routes.

exercises.explain: The correct answer is about attempting to swim at the same spot repeatedly. This demonstrates stalling because the AI is not trying a different approach to solve the problem.
